examples.title: Algorithm: FIFO Page Replacement
examples.code:
ALGORITHM FIFO_Page_Replacement
INPUT: page_references[], num_frames
BEGIN
 Initialize queue to track page order
 Initialize page_faults = 0
 
 FOR EACH page IN page_references:
 IF page NOT IN queue:
 page_faults++
 IF queue.size() == num_frames:
 removed_page = queue.dequeue()
 queue.enqueue(page)
 RETURN page_faults
END
examples.explanation:
1. Queue maintains insertion order
2. Page fault occurs when page not in queue
3. Oldest page removed when frames full
4. New page added to end of queue

examples.title: Algorithm: Optimal Page Replacement
examples.description: Replaces page that won't be used for longest future time
examples.code:
ALGORITHM Optimal_Replacement
INPUT: page_references[], num_frames
BEGIN
 Initialize frames = empty set
 Initialize page_faults = 0
 
 FOR i FROM 0 TO len(page_references)-1:
 IF page_references[i] NOT IN frames:
 page_faults++
 IF len(frames) >= num_frames:
 farthest = -1
 victim_page = -1
 FOR page IN frames:
 found = False
 FOR j FROM i+1 TO len(page_references)-1:
 IF page_references[j] == page:
 IF j > farthest:
 farthest = j
 victim_page = page
 found = True
 BREAK
 IF NOT found:
 victim_page = page
 BREAK
 frames.remove(victim_page)
 frames.add(page_references[i])
 RETURN page_faults
END
examples.explanation:
1. For each page fault, check future references
2. Find page with farthest next use or no future use
3. Replace victim page
4. Requires complete reference string knowledge

examples.title: Algorithm: LRU Page Replacement
examples.description: Implements Least Recently Used policy using counter method
examples.code:
ALGORITHM LRU_Replacement
INPUT: page_references[], num_frames
BEGIN
 Initialize frame_stack = []
 Initialize page_faults = 0
 
 FOR EACH page IN page_references:
 IF page NOT IN frame_stack:
 page_faults++
 IF len(frame_stack) == num_frames:
 removed_page = frame_stack.pop(0)
 frame_stack.append(page)
 ELSE:
 frame_stack.remove(page)
 frame_stack.append(page)
 RETURN page_faults
END
examples.explanation:
1. Stack maintains recent usage order
2. On page hit: move to top
3. On page fault: remove bottom page
4. Stack bottom = least recently used

examples.title: Algorithm: Clock (Second Chance)
examples.description: Approximates LRU using circular buffer and reference bits
examples.code:
ALGORITHM Clock_Replacement
INPUT: page_references[], num_frames
BEGIN
 Initialize circular_buffer = []
 Initialize pointer = 0
 page_faults = 0
 
 FOR EACH page IN page_references:
 IF page NOT IN circular_buffer:
 page_faults++
 WHILE True:
 current = circular_buffer[pointer]
 IF current.reference_bit == 0:
 REPLACE current WITH page
 pointer = (pointer + 1) % num_frames
 BREAK
 ELSE:
 current.reference_bit = 0
 pointer = (pointer + 1) % num_frames
 ELSE:
 UPDATE page's reference_bit = 1
 RETURN page_faults
END
examples.explanation:
1. Circular buffer with reference bits
2. Pointer cycles through pages
3. Page with ref_bit=0 replaced immediately
4. Ref_bit=1 pages get second chance
